message:
Last week I asked my kids where they'd go if there was a fire in our house. My 7-year-old said 'the garage,' my 10-year-old said 'my room to grab my stuff,' and I just… sat there. Realized we'd never actually talked about it.

So we did. We walked around together, picked our meeting spot (the big oak tree out front), talked about not stopping for anything, and I showed them how our smoke alarms work. No drama, just "this is what we do if."

Honestly? They took it way more seriously than I expected. And now I sleep better knowing we're all on the same page.

Have you and your family talked through a fire escape plan together? What made it click for you?
